user_query:
I'm preparing a pull request review for a refactor that touches both Python and JavaScript. Please read the simulated git diff at input/diff.txt (changes from main to feature/refactor-auth) and generate a comprehensive code review checklist that flags security, performance, style, and test coverage issues present in the changes.

Deliverables (write all files under output/):
1) output/review-checklist.json — A machine-readable list of findings. Each finding must include: severity (critical|warning|info), category (security|performance|style|tests), check_id (e.g., SEC-001), message (what's wrong), file (path), line (best-effort line number), and suggestion (an actionable fix). Also include a top-level summary with counts per severity and per category.
2) output/review-checklist.md — A readable review report with sections for Security, Performance, Style, and Tests. Include a summary table with counts by severity and category, and group findings by file with concise explanations and concrete remediation steps.
3) output/pr-template-thorough.md — A thorough, generic PR review template that a reviewer can paste into a PR, with sections for Correctness, Security, Performance, Code Quality, Tests, Documentation, Deployment, and Notes.

Scope and rules:
- Use file-type-specific heuristics for Python and JavaScript. Only report issues supported by the diff.
- Use consistent check IDs when applicable: SEC-001 (hardcoded secret), SEC-002 (SQL injection), SEC-003 (eval/exec usage), SEC-004 (HTTP vs HTTPS), SEC-005 (TLS verification disabled), SEC-006 (command injection), PERF-001 (N+1 query pattern), PERF-003 (string concatenation in loop), STYLE-001 (TODO/FIXME), STYLE-002 (line length >120 chars). Include test coverage notes as their own entries (category "tests").
- Minimum severity threshold is "info"; include info+.
- Call out any new or modified functions lacking tests (e.g., calculate_discount if applicable), and recommend specific test cases.
- Be specific: reference files and lines, explain why each pattern is risky, and give at least one concrete fix suggestion for each issue.

Write the three output files to output/ as specified. If something is ambiguous in the diff, state assumptions in the markdown report.
